text: Machine A can complete a job in 8 hours. Machine B can complete the same job in 12 hours. If both machines work together, how many hours will it take to complete the job?
explanation:
Machine A's rate: 1/8 per hour. Machine B's rate: 1/12 per hour.
Combined rate: 1/8 + 1/12 = 3/24 + 2/24 = 5/24 per hour.
Time to complete job: 1 ÷ (5/24) = 24/5 = 4.8 hours.

Formula shortcut: t = (a×b)/(a+b) = (8×12)/(8+12) = 96/20 = 4.8

text: Worker X can paint a fence in 6 hours. Worker Y can paint the same fence in 9 hours. Working together, how long will it take to paint the fence?
explanation:
Worker X's rate: 1/6 per hour. Worker Y's rate: 1/9 per hour.
Combined rate: 1/6 + 1/9 = 3/18 + 2/18 = 5/18 per hour.
Time: 1 ÷ (5/18) = 18/5 = 3.6 hours.

Shortcut: t = (6×9)/(6+9) = 54/15 = 3.6

text: Machine P can complete a task in 10 hours. Machine Q can complete it in 15 hours. They work together for 2 hours, then Q breaks down. P continues alone. How many additional hours does P need?
explanation:
Combined rate: 1/10 + 1/15 = 3/30 + 2/30 = 5/30 = 1/6 per hour.
Work completed in 2 hours: 2 × 1/6 = 1/3.
Remaining work: 2/3.
P's rate: 1/10 per hour.
Additional time: (2/3) ÷ (1/10) = 20/3 ≈ 6.67 hours.

text: Pipe A fills a pool in 12 hours. Pipe B fills it in 18 hours. A drain empties a full pool in 36 hours. If all three are open, how long to fill the pool?
explanation:
Pipe A rate: 1/12 per hour. Pipe B rate: 1/18 per hour. Drain rate: −1/36 per hour.
Net rate: 1/12 + 1/18 − 1/36 = 3/36 + 2/36 − 1/36 = 4/36 = 1/9 per hour.
Time to fill: 1 ÷ (1/9) = 9 hours.

text: Workers A, B, and C complete a project in 12, 18, and 24 hours respectively. A works alone for 4 hours. Then B joins for 3 hours. Then C joins. How long does C work?
A cistern is filled by pipe A in 20 hours, emptied by pipe B in 30 hours, and filled by pipe C in 40 hours. All three are open and the cistern is 1/4 full. How long to completely fill it?
Pipe A fills a tank in 5 hours. Pipe B fills it in 10 hours. Working together, how long to fill the tank?
A can do a job in 20 days. B can do it in 30 days. They work together for 6 days. What fraction of the work is left?
explanation:
Combined rate=1/20+1/30=3/60+2/60=5/60=1/12 per day.
In 6 days: 6×1/12=1/2 done. Remaining=1/2.

text: Machine X produces 80 widgets/hr, Machine Y produces 120/hr. How long for both to produce 600 widgets together?
If 6 workers build a wall in 10 days, how many days will 15 workers take?
explanation:
Total work=6×10=60 worker-days. With 15: 60/15=4 days.
Inverse proportion: more workers → fewer days.

text: A fills a pool in 8 hrs, B fills it in 12 hrs. A drain empties it in 24 hrs. All open from empty — how long to fill?
A and B together finish a job in 12 days. A alone takes 20 days. How long does B alone take?
A completes a task in 10 hours. B is 50% more efficient than A. How long together?
A reservoir is filled by Inlet A in 20 hrs, emptied by Outlet B in 30 hrs. Both open — how long to fill from empty?
explanation: Net rate=1/20−1/30=3/60−2/60=1/60 per hr. Time=60 hours.

text: A does 2/3 of a job in 16 days, then B finishes the rest in 8 days. How long would both take together?
explanation:
A does 2/3 in 16 days → full job in 24 days. Rate A=1/24.
B does 1/3 in 8 days → full job in 24 days. Rate B=1/24.
Together=1/24+1/24=2/24=1/12. Time=12 days.

text: Pool: Inlet1 fills in 10 hrs, Inlet2 in 30 hrs, Drain empties in 20 hrs. Pool is 1/3 full. All open — time to fill completely?
A takes 3 days more than A+B together, B takes 8 days more than A+B together. How many days does A+B take together?
explanation:
Let t=together time. A alone=t+3, B alone=t+8.
1/(t+3)+1/(t+8)=1/t → t(2t+11)=(t+3)(t+8)=t²+11t+24.
2t²+11t=t²+11t+24 → t²=24 → t=√24=2√6≈4.9 days.

text: 10 men finish a job in 15 days. After 5 days, 5 more men join. How many more days to finish?
explanation:
Total work=10×15=150 man-days. Done in 5 days: 10×5=50.
Remaining=100. Workers now=15. Days=100/15=6.67 days.
